title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, dating app Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to put on free or affordable in-person events for young people. The program is a global expansion of Hinge’s One More Hour social impact initiative aimed at addressing Gen Z loneliness.
bullets: No transcript available; summary based solely on the description.
Hinge announced a $1M fund for social groups.
Target cities: New York, Los Angeles, and London.
Purpose: fund free or affordable in-person social events for young people.
Part of a global expansion of the One More Hour initiative addressing Gen Z loneliness.
takeaways: Note the geographic focus: NY, LA and London are explicitly named—operators in those markets are directly relevant.
The $1M commitment signals a measurable, platform-driven program that will be coordinated locally.
The initiative emphasizes in-person events for Gen Z, so expect Hinge-driven, location-specific activity that local infrastructure stakeholders may want to track.
As a global expansion of an existing program, this reflects an ongoing platform strategy to blend digital and offline engagement in key cities.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates building a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents and connecting it to an AI agent so the agent can understand a specific company's business. It’s presented as an end-to-end, no-graph-expertise-required demo.
bullets: AI agents often lack knowledge of an individual company’s data; knowledge graphs are presented as the solution.
The session shows automatic construction of a knowledge graph from raw documents.
The graph is connected to an agent so the agent can understand and answer questions about your business.
No prior graph theory or graph-query expertise is required.
Includes an end-to-end build demo and links to a related episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB.
takeaways: Knowledge graphs can make AI agents aware of organization-specific information (useful for operations and decision support).
Automated graph construction from documents reduces the need for specialized graph skills on staff.
An end-to-end workflow is shown, indicating a practical path to integrate internal data with AI agents without deep graph expertise.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, explains that energy incentives are only part of the tax picture for sustainability projects and highlights less obvious credits and incentives. She points to state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation (for example reimagining packaging or processes), and underscores that sustainability investments can unlock meaningful tax value when viewed more broadly.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but not the only tax opportunities for sustainability.
State and local abatements can support sustainability initiatives.
Location-based incentives are another underused lever.
R&D tax credits may apply to innovation tied to sustainability (e.g., reimagining packaging or processes).
Viewing sustainability investments through a broader tax lens can unlock meaningful value.
takeaways: Don’t assume energy credits are the only option—identify state/local abatements and location-based incentives relevant to projects.
Consider whether sustainability-driven innovation in operations or processes could qualify for R&D tax credits.
When planning site selection or upgrades, factor in location-based incentives and abatements as part of the financial case.
Framing sustainability investments more broadly can reveal additional tax value opportunities.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the video uses a sky-full-of-stars metaphor as Copilot helps Christa find her inner star. Viewers are invited to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Sky/stars metaphor frames a message of personal discovery.
Copilot is positioned as helping Christa find her “inner star.”
Clear call-to-action: download and try Copilot via link in bio.
Concise, uplifting tone reinforced by a star emoji.
takeaways: Vendor messaging emphasizes end-user empowerment—plan for adoption and training if evaluating the tool.
The social post includes a direct CTA to download—ensure procurement and trial procedures are ready before staff follow the link.
Security and compliance reviews should precede any downloads prompted by social media campaigns.
Short-form promotional content can drive direct installs; track such channels when assessing vendor reach and support needs.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… GeForce Garage highlights the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build, u/JeffersonPutnam, who assembled a small-form-factor PC featuring a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition. The description lists the full parts and links (PCPartPicker) including a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 power supply, and a Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build.
Full system parts list available on PCPartPicker (link provided).
Key components: Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, RTX 5090 Founders Edition, Ryzen 7 9800X3D.
Supporting hardware: MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, 32GB Teamgroup DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
GeForce host: Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato); official GeForce social links included.
takeaways: High-end consumer GPUs like the GeForce RTX 5090 are being specified in compact builds—plan for robust power delivery such as a 1000W SFX unit (Corsair SF1000) when evaluating small-form-factor deployments.
The listed use of a 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) alongside a high-performance CPU and GPU underscores cooling requirements for dense systems.
A current-generation platform (Ryzen 7 9800X3D, DDR5-6000, MSI B850I series motherboard) is shown — expect modern memory and motherboard compatibility considerations for integrating latest GPUs.
Providing a parts list (PCPartPicker) and community engagement examples can simplify BOM validation and reproducibility for standardized builds.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions using Google Cloud's robust infrastructure. Sreeni Alakappan describes how those efforts streamlined operations, boosted developer productivity, compressed the digital transformation timeline, and unlocked new revenue streams.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
The project leveraged GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure to streamline operations.
Developer productivity was improved through the deployed AI solutions.
The engagement compressed TTEC’s digital transformation timeline.
New revenue streams were unlocked as a result of the AI deployment.
takeaways: Robust cloud infrastructure is a foundational requirement for deploying GenAI solutions at scale.
Engaging cloud consulting can compress transformation timelines and reduce time-to-value for AI projects.
Investments that boost developer productivity can accelerate rollout and adoption of AI-driven services.
Rapid prototyping and deployment of AI can open new revenue opportunities tied to customer experience.
